subject: Q1 Order — Golden Coast
body:
Hi Kwame,

Happy new year. We'd like to place our Q1 order of 30 tons standard grade. Can you confirm you can ship before end of January and send over the current pricing?

Thanks,
David

subject: RE: Q1 Order — Golden Coast
body:
David,

Happy new year to you too. Q1 price is $2,780/ton. We can ship the week of January 26, estimated arrival at your facility around February 10-14 depending on port.

Shall I raise the pro-forma invoice?

Kwame

subject: RE: Q1 Order — Golden Coast
body:
Yes please — 30 tons at $2,780/ton. Feb 10-14 works. Send PFI when ready.

David

subject: Pro-Forma Invoice — GCC-2026-0047
body:
David,

Please find attached PFI GCC-2026-0047 for 30 MT standard grade cocoa at USD 2,780/MT. I've cc'd Efua Mensah from our logistics team who will handle the shipment coordination from Tema port.

Please confirm acceptance so we can begin loading.

Kwame

subject: RE: Pro-Forma Invoice — GCC-2026-0047
body:
Confirmed. Proceed with shipment per the PFI.

David

subject: Shipment update — GCC-2026-0047
body:
Hi Efua,

We're past the February 14 estimated arrival and haven't received the shipment or a tracking update. Can you confirm current status?

David

subject: RE: Shipment update — GCC-2026-0047
body:
Hi David,

Apologies for delay in response. The vessel experienced a mechanical issue at sea and was diverted to Las Palmas for inspection. New ETA at your port is approximately March 3-5. Shipping line is MedLink and they are managing the situation. We will send updated B/L once vessel departs Las Palmas.

Efua

subject: Shipment arrived — quality inspection pending
body:
Efua, Kwame,

Shipment arrived this morning. Our QC team is conducting the incoming inspection now — will revert once complete. Note we are already 3+ weeks past original delivery date, which under our contract triggers the delay penalty clause.

David

subject: Quality inspection result — ISSUE
body:
Kwame,

Our QC has completed inspection on GCC-2026-0047 and we have a problem. Moisture content came in at 8.2% on average across the sampled bags (13 bags tested), with two bags as high as 9.1%. Our spec and your contract require <7.5% moisture. This is out of specification.

We need to discuss how you want to handle this — we are not in a position to accept this shipment at contract price. Please respond urgently as this is holding up production.

David

subject: RE: Quality inspection result — ISSUE
body:
David,

Thank you for the update. We take quality seriously and I have reviewed this with our team.

I must respectfully push back on your findings. Our pre-shipment inspection at Tema port on January 24 showed moisture at 6.8% — well within spec. The moisture reading of 8.2% is not consistent with what we shipped. We believe the issue may be related to the vessel diversion and the time spent at Las Palmas — extended transit in a non-standard hold environment.

We do not consider this a Golden Coast quality failure. We shipped compliant product. We would request an independent third-party moisture test before discussing any price adjustment.

Kwame

subject: RE: Quality inspection result — ISSUE
body:
Kwame,

We understand your position, but we do not have a copy of your January 24 pre-shipment moisture report. Can you send that documentation? 

We are open to an independent test but need to agree on the testing lab and who bears the cost. Our position is that the product arrived out of spec, and that is what we took delivery of. We are using this batch under protest while we resolve the dispute, but we will not pay the full contract price for non-conforming goods.

David

subject: RE: Quality inspection result — pre-shipment cert attached
body:
David,

Please see the attached pre-shipment moisture certificate from SGS Ghana (cert ref: SGS-GH-2026-00312, dated January 24). Result: 6.8% moisture, within spec.

We propose SGS Netherlands for the independent test. Costs to be split equally. If the result confirms our position (i.e. the product was in spec when shipped), we expect full payment at contract price plus the cost of the independent test.

Kwame

subject: RE: Quality dispute — update
body:
Kwame,

We have reviewed the SGS Ghana certificate. We are not disputing that the product may have been within spec at loading, but our receipt test is what governs under the contract — risk passes at destination, not at origin. We are still reviewing the contract language with our legal team.

On SGS Netherlands — we can agree to that lab but need to clarify the split only covers the test cost, not any broader liability conclusion. We'll revert early next week.

David
